LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAY-19-2020 HEARING HELD RE SAUNDREA MABREY'S MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT. MATTER DISCUSSED. THE COURT INSTRUCTS COUNSEL TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION FROM THE SETTLEMENT ADMINISTRATOR BY JUNE 2, 2020 THAT ADDRESSES THE NOTICE ISSUE. DEPENDING ON THE CONTENTS OF THAT DECLARATION, THE COURT WILL EITHER DEEM THE MATTER SUBMITTED ON JUNE 2, 2020 AND WILL GRANT FINAL APPROVAL, THE FEE REQUEST, AND SERVICE AWARD REQUEST WITHOUT THE NEED TO CONDUCT A FURTHER HEARING, OR SET A FURTHER HEARING DATE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2020 Continued To Sep-02-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Final Approval Hearing And Any Hearing On Motions For Attorneys' Fees And Service Award (Per Order Filed 12/10/19)
Off Calendar May-19-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Scott J. Hyman of Severson & Werson, APC (Tel: 949-442-7110) appearing via CourtCall for Plaintiff & Cross-Defendants Autovest LLC, Universal Acceptance Corporation, and Interstate Auto Group, Inc.;
Kristin Kemnitzer, Esq. of Kemnitzer, Barron & Krieg, LLP (Tel: 415-632-1900) appearing via CourtCall for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Saundrea Mabrey.

HEARING RE SAUNDREA MABREY'S MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. The Court and counsel discuss the settlement administrator's declaration (Exhibit #3 to the Declaration of Kristin Kemnizter in Support of Motion for Final Approval of Class Action Settlement). The Court reads the declaration as indicating that nearly one-quarter of the class members did not receive actual notice of the settlement. The Court instructs counsel as follows: By June 2, 2020, counsel are to file a supplemental declaration from the settlement administrator that addresses this notice issue. If the supplemental declaration is able to clarify that the Court misread the intent of the original declaration, the Court will deem the matter submitted on June 2, 2020 and will grant final approval, the fee request, and service award request without the need to conduct a further hearing. If the supplemental declaration confirms that the Court's reading of the original declaration was correct and that nearly one-quarter of the class members did not receive actual notice of the settlement, counsel for parties are to file an additional one page document. That document is to provide the Court with a timeline for locating the class members who have not yet received notice, sending out notices to those class members, receiving responses back from those class members, and a proposed further hearing date to occur sometime after the deadline for responses. If counsel believe locating the unreached class members is not reasonable or feasible, counsel must explain to the Court why this is the case.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

If the supplemental declaration confirms that the Court's reading of the original declaration was correct and that nearly one-quarter of the class members did not receive actual notice of the settlement, counsel for parties are to file an additional one page document. That document is to provide the Court with a timeline for locating the class members who have not yet received notice, sending out notices to those class members, receiving responses back from those class members, and a proposed further hearing date to occur sometime after the deadline for responses. If counsel believe locating the unreached class members is not reasonable or feasible, counsel must explain to the Court why this is the case.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott J. Hyman, Esq. of SEVERSON & WERSON, T:949.225.7951, for plaintiff/cross defendant Autovest;
Kristin Kemnitzer, Esq. of KEMNITZER, BARRON & KRIEG, LLP, T:415.632.1900, for defendant/cross complainant Saundrea Mabrey.

RULING: There being no opposition and finding good cause, the Court ruled as follows:

DEFENDANT SAUNDREA MABREY's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement is GRANTED. Parties stipulated to the briefing schedule re: final approval hearing. Final Approval Hearing is set for May 19, 2020 at 9:30am in Department 302.

Order signed. See order for details.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
